jokes.text: If it's positive, 'Awesome! We're having a baby! I wanna name it Frisbee!' If it's negative, 'Awesome. We're not gonna fight about the name Frisbee, and we get disco fries for breakfast 'cause we're sad.'
jokes.setup: Waiting for pregnancy test results, classic setup for nervous energy
jokes.punchline: The absurd name 'Frisbee' and weirdly specific backup plan of disco fries
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurdist setup - takes normal situation and makes specific absurd choices. 'Frisbee' as baby name is perfectly random, disco fries as consolation prize is wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: All those years trying to get on 'America's Funniest Home Videos'. Aah! Oh! Aah!
jokes.setup: Liz being envious of someone else's pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's been intentionally hurting herself for TV show submissions
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - reveals Liz's desperate attempts at fame through self-injury, which is perfectly on-brand for her character.

jokes.text: What kind of idiot gives the little kid orange soda?
jokes.punchline: Focusing on wrong parenting concern given Dennis's obvious unfitness
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor showing Liz's misplaced priorities - worried about sugar instead of obvious bigger issues.

jokes.text: I've got a brand-new business selling suicide insurance, and it's going great.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his new business venture
jokes.punchline: The logically impossible concept of suicide insurance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis line - combines his stupidity with dark humor. The business model makes no logical sense but Dennis delivers it with confidence. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Obviously, he's gonna be an athlete, so I only got to save up for, like, one year of college.
jokes.setup: Dennis talking about his adopted baby's future
jokes.punchline: Racist assumption combined with ignorance about athletic scholarships
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis - casually racist assumptions delivered with complete confidence in his flawed logic. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: You remember Megan Duffy, maiden name Duffy. Hopefully no relation.
jokes.punchline: Her maiden name and married name are the same, suggesting she married a relative
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay joke with perfect economy. The 'hopefully no relation' sells it as Dennis being oblivious to the implications. Well-structured punchline.

jokes.text: I recognize you two. Did we have a four-way?
jokes.punchline: Casually asking about group sex as if trying to place where they met
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor - treating extremely inappropriate topic as casual small talk. Good economy and shock value.

jokes.text: We decided to adopt 'cause Megan's real career-oriented right now, didn't want to wreck her boobs.
jokes.punchline: Reducing pregnancy to its effect on breasts, delivered as if it's thoughtful
jokes.explanation: Typical Dennis - reducing complex decisions to crude physical concerns, delivered with his characteristic obliviousness to how inappropriate he sounds.

jokes.text: Couple months later, boom, we got Black Dennis.
jokes.punchline: Named the baby 'Black Dennis' as if it's perfectly normal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Dennis - casually racist naming convention delivered without any awareness of how wrong it is. Strong character specificity.

jokes.text: Yeah, right, Liz, the guy with the black son is racist.
jokes.punchline: Using adoption as shield from racism accusation while being obviously racist
jokes.explanation: Sharp irony - Dennis using his adoption as proof he's not racist while literally being racist. Good structure and character work.

jokes.text: Later days, dummies. 21st-century family coming through! This is the new normal!
jokes.punchline: Appropriating progressive language while being regressive himself
jokes.explanation: Dennis trying to sound progressive while being anything but. The irony of him representing 'new normal' is effective character comedy.

jokes.text: I read the one about a handsome genius who lied to two idiots about reading scripts.
jokes.setup: Asked if he read movie scripts
jokes.punchline: Describes Tracy's actual situation as if it's a movie plot
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta joke - Dotcom describing the actual situation as if it's a fictional movie. Great economy and self-aware humor typical of 30 Rock's style.

jokes.text: Dammit-I'll-Pick-A-Name-Later Dotcom Productions.
jokes.punchline: The company name literally admits they haven't thought of a name yet
jokes.explanation: Clever naming joke that's both lazy and self-aware about being lazy. Good economy and fits Tracy's impulsive character.

jokes.text: While on holiday in Tuscany, a middle-aged divorcée falls in love with a lazy pervert who's into hats.
jokes.setup: Typical romantic movie premise in Tuscany
jokes.punchline: The romantic interest is a 'lazy pervert who's into hats'
jokes.explanation: Subverts romantic movie clichés with absurd character description. The hat detail is wonderfully random and specific.

jokes.text: In a world with too much freedom, one young man dared to be different. 'Hitler: The Boy Who Dreamed of Stars'!
jokes.setup: Epic movie trailer voice describing inspirational story
jokes.punchline: It's about Hitler, presented as feel-good biography
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - uses inspirational movie language for Hitler biography. The 'dreamed of stars' detail makes it even more absurd. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: It's a Harriet Tubman biopic entitled, 'The Moses of Maryland'. I'll buy it! Sorry, I was talking on my Bluetooth to a guy at the octopus auction.
jokes.setup: Tracy seems to approve of serious historical biopic
jokes.punchline: He was talking to someone else about buying octopi
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - seems like character growth moment but reveals Tracy's distraction. 'Octopus auction' is wonderfully specific and absurd.

jokes.text: How would you like to be the voice of Gaboing, the evil wizard who would take jumping away from Jumpland?
jokes.setup: Offering Tracy a voice acting role
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous concept and made-up word 'Gaboing'
jokes.explanation: Silly children's movie parody with nonsense names and concepts. 'Gaboing' is a good made-up villain name.

jokes.text: You know who else was married? Ted Bundy. - I don't think so, Liz. - Really? He's so handsome. Is he still alive?
jokes.setup: Liz arguing that marriage doesn't make someone a good parent
jokes.punchline: She's attracted to Ted Bundy and doesn't know he's dead
jokes.explanation: Good Liz character moment - her terrible judgment about men extends to serial killers. The ignorance about his death status adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I ignore it when you eat popsicles by biting them with your teeth, which is insane!
jokes.punchline: Criss has the weird habit of biting popsicles
jokes.explanation: Relatable relationship humor - everyone has weird habits their partner tolerates. The specificity makes it funny.

jokes.text: Me need piece of paper to know how I feel! Guhhhh, Liz marry Criss? Doy! Errr, all right.
jokes.setup: Liz getting frustrated about marriage requirements
jokes.punchline: She devolves into caveman speech and gestures
jokes.explanation: Liz's tendency to regress under stress shown through caveman impression. Good physical comedy and character consistency.

jokes.text: 'Top Gun'-style. Jenna Maroney, you are my slave.
jokes.punchline: Claims to own Jenna as a slave
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from 'Top Gun' entrance to slavery claim. Sets up the Surge Cola storyline effectively.

jokes.text: For a million points, he got me! Surge! Suck it down!
jokes.setup: Explaining the Surge Cola commercial escalation
jokes.punchline: She was literally a prize in the promotion
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist premise - person as promotional prize. The slogan delivery adds period-appropriate energy. Sets up episode's B-plot nicely.

jokes.text: My boyfriend was supposed to pick me up after that shoot, so I called him, and I was like, 'O.J., where are you?' And he was like, 'Wait, you're alive? Then who did I kill?'
jokes.setup: Jenna talking about calling her boyfriend in 1994
jokes.punchline: It was O.J. Simpson, confused about his murder timing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy mixing real events with Jenna's inflated sense of importance. Perfect timing with the 1994 setting. The confused O.J. line is incredibly quotable and shocking.

jokes.text: Well, you do have the wind-battered face of a New England cod fisherman, if that's what you're asking.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if she looks glowing
jokes.punchline: Jack's brutal assessment of her appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack line - specific, cutting, and delivered with his characteristic eloquence. The fisherman comparison is beautifully brutal and specific.

jokes.text: I'll get Tony Bennett to sing. I saved his life at an illegal pai gow game once. He owes me a favor.
jokes.setup: Jack offering to make Liz's wedding special
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions saving Tony Bennett at illegal gambling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack backstory - mixes high culture (Tony Bennett) with illegal activities. Delivered as throwaway line which makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I had forgotten to stock up on toilet paper, but I had remembered to stock up on seven-layer dip --
jokes.setup: Liz explaining what she did with her wedding dress during Hurricane Irene
jokes.punchline: She prioritized party food over basic necessities
jokes.explanation: Great Liz character moment - her terrible priorities and food obsession. The contrast between emergency prep and party snacks is perfect.

jokes.text: Sol Rosenbear and I would like to thank everyone for coming to our destination wedding here on the Love Boat. Unfortunately, Sol has discovered my previous relationship with Gopher, so he will be seeking an annulment once we reach Puerto Vallarta.
jokes.punchline: It's on Love Boat and ends in annulment due to affair with Gopher
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - even Liz's childhood wedding fantasies are cynical and involve infidelity. The Love Boat setting and Gopher affair are perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Maybe there's not gonna be a wedding! You're ruining my special day! Mom, what the [beep] are you wearing?
jokes.punchline: The show immediately demonstrates her point about selfish behavior
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke - the show immediately proves Liz's point about wedding behavior. The escalation from wedding to family fighting is effective.

jokes.text: So tomorrow Liz Lemon is getting married in a sweatshirt, no bra!
jokes.punchline: Specific details of her deliberately unstylish wedding outfit
jokes.explanation: Good Liz character moment - her rebellion involves being deliberately frumpy. The 'no bra' detail makes her stance more extreme.

jokes.text: We are registered at Popcorn Palace!
jokes.punchline: Her registry is at a popcorn store instead of traditional places
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz touch - her wedding registry reflects her food obsessions. 'Popcorn Palace' is a wonderfully specific choice that fits her character.

jokes.text: I guess we've replaced so much of you over the years that you're maybe not you anymore, but you're healthy.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman giving Tracy test results
jokes.punchline: So much of Tracy has been medically replaced he might not be the same person
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dr. Spaceman line - casual delivery of existential horror. The idea that medical replacements make you not yourself is both funny and disturbing.

jokes.text: I always assumed I'd die young because, you know, I look so much like James Dean.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why being healthy is bad news
jokes.punchline: He thinks he looks like James Dean
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy delusion - he sees resemblance to James Dean where none exists. Fits his pattern of grandiose self-image.

jokes.text: That's why I'm always breaking the law, buying exotic sharks, forgetting to feed them, and then trying to hug them!
jokes.punchline: The progression from law-breaking to shark-hugging
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy escalation - starts with general law-breaking and ends with specific shark neglect and attempted affection. Great absurdist progression.

jokes.text: The money I've made off your health problems has put me almost all the way through medical school.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman explaining why Tracy's health is bad for him
jokes.punchline: He's been profiting from Tracy's problems to pay for medical school
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about medical ethics - Dr. Spaceman admits to exploiting patient for education funding. The casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Drink eight glasses a day of that stuff. You know, clear bathtub juice.
jokes.punchline: Calls water 'clear bathtub juice'
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy wordplay - 'clear bathtub juice' for water is absurd but logical from his perspective. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: But I do have a pamphlet that has a bunch of drawings of vaginae.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman saying he doesn't have helpful pamphlets
jokes.punchline: Offers inappropriate medical pamphlet instead
jokes.explanation: Typical Dr. Spaceman inappropriate medical practice. The plural 'vaginae' adds to the clinical absurdity.

jokes.text: I'm moving to Arizona to be with my new slave master Terry to live in a birdcage he built.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why she needs out of her contract
jokes.punchline: She's willingly entering a bizarre relationship arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna absurdity - she treats weird relationship as normal career move. The birdcage detail adds to the bizarreness.

jokes.text: I've calculated that she is worth $800,000.
jokes.setup: Terry doing math on Jenna's value
jokes.punchline: Treats human being like financial asset with specific calculation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the negotiation scene effectively. The specific dollar amount treats Jenna like commodity, which fits the absurd premise.

jokes.text: Uh, let's say Jenna Was worth that in her prime. That was 10 years ago.
jokes.punchline: Casually states her prime was a decade ago
jokes.explanation: Good Jack negotiation tactic delivered with brutal honesty. The casual cruelty is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: A 7th-grade education, hepatitis D, bullet in jaw, fatwa, credit card debt, wanted by the Yakuza --
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and dangerous problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from basic education problems to international criminal organizations. Each item gets progressively more absurd and dangerous.

jokes.punchline: Her total value is shockingly low
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline delivery - the pause and low amount after all that buildup. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: In Arizona, two grand will buy me a castle and a pillowcase full of meth.
jokes.punchline: His Arizona plans involve drugs and grandiose living
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Terry's priorities are clearly skewed. The specific 'pillowcase full of meth' is a nice detail.

jokes.text: I used to only say stuff like that in my white nerd voice.
jokes.setup: Tracy speaking formally about market analysis
jokes.punchline: Admits he usually reserves intelligence for his 'white nerd voice'
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on Tracy's character development and racial stereotypes. Self-aware humor about his own performance of intelligence.

jokes.text: Oh! I see you over there, you little gumdrop. Don't hide from Shanice!
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate workplace flirtation with pet names
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from inappropriate workplace behavior. 'Gumdrop' is a nice specific pet name choice.

jokes.text: Just like the Hiltons!
jokes.setup: Talking about becoming amazing parents through marriage
jokes.punchline: References the Hiltons as parenting examples
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reference - the Hiltons are notorious for poor parenting outcomes. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: I'll give you a bath in the sink like a baby!
jokes.setup: Shanice continuing to flirt with Criss
jokes.punchline: Escalates to inappropriate domestic fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of inappropriate workplace behavior. The specific imagery is both nurturing and creepy.

jokes.text: Yeah, 'cause we were wearing them when we first -- Met. Met.
jokes.punchline: Almost reveals they were having sex when they met
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with awkward recovery. The repetition of 'Met' sells the awkwardness effectively.

jokes.text: You know, I always imagined getting married right when they take your picture on Splash Mountain.
jokes.setup: Criss talking about his wedding dreams
jokes.punchline: Wants to get married at the exact moment of a theme park photo
jokes.explanation: Great Criss character moment - perfectly childlike and specific. The timing element (right when photo is taken) makes it uniquely absurd.

jokes.text: When they said, 'You may now kiss the bride,' I did my first ever eye-roll.
jokes.setup: Liz remembering her first wedding as flower girl
jokes.punchline: She was cynical about marriage even as a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz origin story - shows her cynicism started early. The eye-roll as milestone is perfectly character-specific.

jokes.text: Are you kidding me, Chros?
jokes.punchline: She mispronounces his name when angry
jokes.explanation: Good subtle character moment - stress affects her pronunciation. Shows relationship dynamics through small details.

jokes.text: Oh, also, I told him you're Italian, so he might call you Tina --
jokes.punchline: Made up ethnicity for Liz based on stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character moment - casual manipulation of facts for his own purposes. Shows his tendency to manage situations.

jokes.text: I work at the Marriage Bureau, but I'll never get married.
jokes.punchline: Her job has made her cynical about marriage
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about workplace cynicism. The irony is clear and relatable.

jokes.text: I'm gonna hold you to that, sir!
jokes.punchline: Kenneth treats it as a business commitment
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character moment - his eagerness to accept any valuation. Shows his desperate need for validation.

jokes.text: I'm at an age where I no longer need erectile softeners.
jokes.setup: Jack listing his own declining value
jokes.punchline: Overshares about sexual dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Good Jack vulnerability moment with TMI element. The 'softeners' instead of 'hardeners' is a nice detail.

jokes.text: I coined the phrase, 'You wish, pal!'
jokes.punchline: Claims to have invented common phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack delusion - claiming credit for mundane phrase as major achievement. Fits his pattern of grandiose self-regard.

jokes.text: Mazel tov, dummies. You're Jewish, right, Liz?
jokes.punchline: Makes assumption about Liz's religion based on appearance
jokes.explanation: Typical Dennis inappropriate assumption. The casual delivery of offensive stereotype is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Getting my show picked up, or meeting you, or the time I got a sleeve of Starburst with all pink!
jokes.setup: Liz listing more important days than her wedding
jokes.punchline: Includes candy as equal to major life events
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz priorities - candy ranks with major life achievements. The specificity of 'all pink' Starburst makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: My parents spent the money they saved up for my wedding on a PT Cruiser.
jokes.punchline: Parents chose notoriously ugly car over daughter's wedding
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail - PT Cruiser is perfect choice for terrible purchase. Shows Liz's family's poor priorities and taste.

jokes.text: Ergo. Affleck's finally gonna get that Oscar.
jokes.punchline: Dennis completely misinterprets it as reference to Ben Affleck
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dennis moment - mishears 'ergo' as 'Argo' and makes film prediction. Perfect character-specific stupidity with great economy.

jokes.text: She made it out of parachute silk while hiding from the Germans.
jokes.punchline: Reveals dramatic WWII backstory
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from simple dress to dramatic wartime story. The specificity makes Liz look terrible for her previous dismissal.

jokes.text: Oh, Rosenbear. He never got enough time in his garden before he passed.
jokes.setup: Liz looking at wedding planning materials
jokes.punchline: Reveals emotional attachment to fictional childhood wedding groom
jokes.explanation: Beautiful callback that adds depth to earlier joke. Liz's genuine emotion for fictional character shows her romantic nature.

jokes.text: And you're gonna wear a suit that makes you look like a little marzipan candy man!
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and adorable description of Criss in formal wear
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz line - 'marzipan candy man' is incredibly specific and endearing. Shows her affection through food metaphors.

jokes.text: It's my special day!
jokes.setup: Liz fully embracing wedding planning after rejecting it
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal to wedding cliché behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect character reversal - Liz becomes everything she criticized. The delivery shows complete transformation.

jokes.text: Tracy, it's me, Harriet Tubman.
jokes.setup: Tracy having some kind of vision or dream
jokes.punchline: Harriet Tubman appears but looks/sounds like Jack
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment - historical figure appearing in Tracy's mind but looking like his boss. Sets up meta-commentary on his mental state.

jokes.text: I grew up Interior Maryland slave shack, the year 18.
jokes.setup: Fake Harriet Tubman explaining her background
jokes.punchline: Reads directly from script formatting, including incomplete date
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - character literally reads stage directions as dialogue. Perfect callback to Tracy not reading scripts properly.

jokes.text: Eat a corn on the cob and make it sexy!
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to control his dream/vision
jokes.punchline: Bizarre and inappropriate request for historical figure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdity - sexualizing food and making inappropriate demands of historical figures. Very character-specific weirdness.

jokes.text: I could forget my chimpanzee's birthday!
jokes.setup: Tracy listing ways he could still die
jokes.punchline: Owns a chimpanzee that apparently has dangerous birthday expectations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy absurdity - owns dangerous pet and treats its birthday as life-threatening obligation. Perfect escalation from mundane to bizarre.

jokes.text: Liz Lemon's getting married? Harry Truman was right! Anything's possible!
jokes.punchline: Attributes random presidential quote to event
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character moment - connects unrelated historical figure to current event. Shows his scattered knowledge and dramatic tendencies.

jokes.text: A super-expensive period piece starring a middle-aged woman? No one's gonna want to see it!
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why the Harriet Tubman movie is perfect
jokes.punchline: Accurate assessment of box office reality for historical films
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on Hollywood's commercial realities. Tracy's assessment is unfortunately accurate about historical dramas.

jokes.text: Black Dennis, start the car!
jokes.punchline: Treats baby as chauffeur
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates Dennis's inappropriate treatment of his adopted son. Shows his complete lack of parenting awareness.

jokes.text: What? It's the only white dress I own.
jokes.punchline: Her 'wedding dress' is clearly inappropriate for weddings
jokes.explanation: Good Liz practicality moment - solves wedding dress problem with literal interpretation. Shows her fashion cluelessness.

jokes.text: But your hair! I'm a princess!
jokes.punchline: Liz fully embraced princess fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - Liz has completely embraced the wedding fantasy she previously rejected. Shows her transformation.

jokes.text: Tito was a drug dealer the police shot in the face. In Riverside Park. Where we first met.
jokes.punchline: Wedding rings came from violent crime at romantic location
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy juxtaposition - romantic gesture with violent backstory. The location connection makes it even more twisted.

jokes.text: 'But you see,' said Roark quietly, 'I've chosen the work I want to do. And I can find joy only if I do my work in the best way possible to me.'
jokes.punchline: Quotes from Ayn Rand's 'The Fountainhead' instead of traditional vows
jokes.explanation: Good intellectual humor - using objectivist philosophy at wedding ceremony. Shows the officiant's pretentious or confused priorities.

jokes.text: Oh! My lip is caught in the grill!
jokes.punchline: Romantic moment ruined by dental work accident
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy that deflates romantic moment. Fits the show's tendency to undercut sentimental moments with absurdity.

jokes.text: You know, I'm attractive. I've got cheekbones and a pair you can do something with.
jokes.punchline: Objectifies his own body parts for dating purposes
jokes.explanation: Good Jack vanity moment with awkward self-assessment. The clinical way he describes his assets is both confident and creepy.

jokes.text: Black Dennis got some cop's gun!
jokes.punchline: Baby somehow acquired a weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and callback - the baby has become dangerous. Dark comedy about child safety and Dennis's negligence.
